summary:
White hair appears predominantly in this class due likely to shared genes; no similar cases exist locally but only amongst some students, pointing towards inherited traits over environment-specific causes. \boxed{Genetics}
The high prevalence specifically among certain individuals suggests it could be related to their common ancestry or inheritance pattern more than any general factor affecting everyone equally across locations like diet or pollution exposure. Thus, we conclude that **genetic predisposition** best accounts for these observations given all provided information about distribution patterns seen here compared against broader populations without such symptoms present nearby geographically speaking too far apart logically making random mutation unlikely as sole cause either. Therefore my final answer would still stand at option B reflecting strong evidence supporting familial linkages being primary driver behind observed phenomenon despite needing further testing perhaps through family tree analysis etc...
